mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises by >1.5% in 1 month but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.6% signals tight labor markets, which drives wage inflation and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: UNRATE falls below 3.6% but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in >50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong demand and supply-side momentum, creating a positive feedback loop for equity markets over 60 days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise by >1% but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) pressures central banks to accelerate rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 260 (CPIAUCSL) triggers market expectations of prolonged monetary tightening, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 260 but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1.5%) reduces borrowing costs for corporations, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5% but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in >40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market, driving consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate strong demand-side momentum, creating tailwinds for equity markets over 20 days.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both rise but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in >40% of cases.
